title: periploca visciformis
intro: Periploca visciformis, also known as Poisonous Periploca, is a woody, deciduous climbing vine native to tropical and subtropical regions of the world. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce inflammation and act as an antiseptic, antiviral, and antibacterial agent. The health benefits of Periploca visciformis include its potential to alleviate fever, improve respiratory issues, and treat various skin conditions. Herbal preparations of Periploca visciformis typically involve infusions, decoctions, or tinctures made from its leaves, roots, or stems, although the plant's toxicity requires careful handling and dosing.
